case.title: JORDAN v. VILLAGE OF MENOMONEE FALLS

opinion.h1: JORDAN v. VILLAGE OF MENOMONEE FALLS, 385 U.S. 4 (1966)
opinion.h4: JORDAN ET AL v. VILLAGE OF MENOMONEE FALLS. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF WISCONSIN. No. 122. Decided October 10, 1966.
opinion.p: Emil Hersh, Arthur Magidson, Joseph A. Fanelli and James H. Mann for appellants.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
385 U.S. 4 BENNETT v. UNITED STATES. ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF CERTIORARI TO THE UNITED STATES COURT OF CLAIMS. No. 143. Decided October 10, 1966.
Certiorari granted; 174 Ct. Cl. 492, 356 F.2d 525, vacated and remanded.
Solicitor General Marshall for the United States.

In light of the suggestion of the Solicitor General and upon an examination of the papers filed in this case, the petition for a writ of certiorari is granted, the judgment of the United States Court of Claims is vacated and the case is remanded to that court.
